rationale: The cited 2009 paper studies trustworthiness reputations and communication under one-sided information asymmetry in channel negotiations, which conceptually overlaps with the 2024 paper’s focus on trust/dependence under potentially misaligned B2B supplier interests. However, the provided evidence includes only a DOI-level reference match (no in-text citation context or reference text), so there is not enough direct evidence to confirm substantive use versus background mention.
